message:
fix: correct four runtime correctness bugs found during deep audit

TaskTable stored_future leak (task_table.rs):
- remove() and remove_task() now clean up the stored_futures HashMap
  entry when removing a task record, preventing unbounded memory growth
  when tasks are removed but their stored futures linger in the map
- Added regression test: remove_task_cleans_stored_future

Hedge backup spawn failure orphans primary (scope.rs):
- When the backup spawn fails in hedge() after the primary is already
  running, the primary task is now explicitly abort-cancelled with
  CancelReason::resource_unavailable() instead of silently returning
  an error while leaving the primary task running unmonitored
- Used let-else pattern for cleaner control flow
- Added regression test: hedge_backup_spawn_failure_aborts_primary
- Added CancelKind and RegionLimits imports for test coverage

Duration-to-nanos overflow in LabRuntime chaos injection (runtime.rs):
- advance_time() previously cast Duration::as_nanos() (u128) directly
  to u64 via `as`, silently truncating durations longer than ~584 years
  which can occur with randomized chaos delay injection
- Added duration_nanos_saturating() helper that clamps via
  u64::try_from().unwrap_or(u64::MAX) for correct saturation
- Removed unused Severity import
- Added regression test: duration_nanos_saturating_clamps_large_duration

Incomplete PartialEq in CrashPack/FailureInfo (crashpack.rs):
- FailureInfo::eq() now includes the `outcome` field, which was
  previously omitted — two failures with different outcomes (Panic vs
  Err) at the same (task, region, time) were incorrectly considered
  equal
- CrashPack::eq() now includes `attachments`, `divergent_prefix`,
  `evidence`, `supervision_log`, and `replay` fields that were
  previously omitted, meaning structurally different crash packs could
  compare as equal
- Updated existing test assertions to reflect corrected semantics
- Added regression test: crash_pack_inequality_on_different_divergent_prefix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
